name: Adam Nussbaum
abstract: Adam Nussbaum (born November 29, 1955) is an American jazz drummer.

interviews.title: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

concerts.label: CareFusion Jazz Festival New York: A Salute to James Moody

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve played with you know Billy Hart and just Peter Erskine, Adam Nussbaum
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Adam Nussbaum had been hanging around me since Elvin... Adam was on line... You got to work with me all night... That’s how that band became him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Bergonzi
about_quotes.evidence: I’m playing with the Elvin Jones 50th Anniversary of Live at the Lighthouse with Dave Liebman, Gene Perla, Adam Nussbaum.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Bergonzi interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 7, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Mark Soskin
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve also worked with Adam Nussbaum a bunch. And so you know I’ve used Adam when I’ve gone on tour
about_quotes.doc_title: Mark Soskin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 17, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: What is – the drummer’s name is? – the other drummer’s name is Adam Nussbaum, I think. He had the second part of the club.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: He took a lesson but I didn’t teach him. Adam Nussbaum was curious but I didn’t think he was serious.
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
